_comment: Moment scoring weights per brand category. Range: -20 (strong negative signal) to +20 (strong positive signal). Zero means neutral/no meaningful effect.

quick-service-restaurant._rationale: Driven by commute patterns and meal-occasion timing. Bad weather pushes toward fast/convenient food rather than cooking. Morning and lunch are peak QSR dayparts by revenue.

coffee._rationale: Morning routine is the dominant signal — strongest category-time alignment in the set. Cold and rainy conditions are classic behavioral triggers for hot beverage purchase. Weekday commuter habits are a major driver.

delivery-food._rationale: Bad weather is the single strongest demand driver — people substitute delivery for going out. Evening is peak order time. Weekends add a leisure/laziness multiplier. Clear skies suppress demand as people venture out.

grocery._rationale: Weekend shopping trips are the dominant behavioral pattern. Nice weather encourages running errands. Severe weather suppresses trips (except panic-buying, which is pre-event). After-work afternoon/evening slots are the weekday peak.

auto-insurance._rationale: Risk salience drives consideration — rain and snow make hazardous driving conditions top of mind. Commute hours activate car-related thinking. Weekdays dominate because that is when driving exposure is highest.

ride-share._rationale: Late-night bar/event traffic is the single strongest signal — safe-ride-home intent peaks here. Any bad weather strongly suppresses willingness to walk or wait at a stop. Clear-sky weather encourages walking and cycling instead.

sporting-goods._rationale: Clear weather is the primary inspiration trigger — it prompts activity planning and gear browsing. Weekend is when active consumers shop. Snow is a positive signal only for winter-sport gear (net moderate positive). Rain strongly suppresses outdoor-activity intent.

outdoor-apparel._rationale: Cold is the dominant purchase trigger — nothing sells parkas like a cold snap. Snow amplifies this. Wind drives layering and shell purchases. Weekends are primary shopping days for the outdoor lifestyle consumer.

home-improvement._rationale: Weekend warrior projects are the defining behavior — strongest weekday/weekend delta in the set. Clear sky is essential for most outdoor work. Severe winter weather shuts down project intent almost entirely. Afternoon is peak hardware store traffic.
